- Metadata only -- Configuration and account data without conversation content
3. Choose the format: JSONL or CSV
4. Optionally check Include blobs to include binary attachments and files
5. Click Queue Export

Exports are processed in the background. You can navigate away and check back later.

Export lifecycle

| Status | Meaning |
|---|---|
| Queued | Waiting to start |
| Running | Currently being processed |
| Completed | Ready to download |
| Failed | Job errored -- you can retry |
| Expired | Old exports are automatically cleaned up after the retention period |

Downloading exports

Once an export shows "Completed":

1. Click on the export to view details
2. Download the manifest file and individual data files
3. Each file includes a checksum for integrity verification

What's next

- Knowledge Files -- Manage your uploaded documents
- Memory Vault -- View and manage persistent memories
- Billing -- Manage your subscription

title: Tools
body:
Tools

FarmClaw tools are delivered through skills. Each skill gives your assistant a focused capability and the workflow needed to execute it reliably.

Tool inventory

The current built-in tool set includes three skills:

| Tool | Skill ID | What it does | Best for |
|---|---|---|---|
| Grain Bids | farm-grain-bids | Visits your configured elevator pages and extracts current cash bids. | Price checks, cross-elevator comparisons, and finding the best bid by commodity |
| Weather Forecasts | farm-weather | Pulls forecast and alert data for your farm location, then adds ag-focused interpretation. | Morning weather briefings, spray-window checks, and short-term field planning |
| Scale Tickets | farm-scale-tickets | Records delivery tickets from photo, voice, or manual entry and stores them in your farm's database. | Harvest logging, load history, bushel totals, and export-ready records |

These tool IDs are the same IDs used in FarmClaw configuration and agent assignment (farm-grain-bids, farm-weather, farm-scale-tickets).

How tools are managed

Tool access follows the same two-step model as skills:

1. Global toggle in Settings > Skills controls whether the tool is enabled for the workspace.
2. Per-agent assignment in Settings > Agents controls which agents can use each enabled tool.

An explicit inventory of tools plus clear policy on how access is controlled.

Additional tools

Beyond the three built-in skills, your workspace includes several supplementary tools:

| Tool | What it does |
|---|---|
| Custom Skills | Create your own skills with AI-assisted generation |
| Knowledge Files | Upload documents your assistant can reference |
| Memory Vault | Persistent facts and context across conversations |
| Email | Send and receive email through your assistant |
| Secrets Vault | Store API keys for authenticated external API calls |
| Spending Cap | Set monthly usage limits |

Related docs

- Skills Overview -- Detailed behavior and configuration for each built-in skill
- Skills & Scheduling -- Assign tools per agent and configure cron jobs
- MCP Servers -- Custom tool servers for advanced use cases
- Visual Workflow Builder -- Guided automation authoring walkthrough
- Workflow Builder Rollout Rings -- Canary/standard/delayed release controls
- Workflow KPI Review Cadence -- Weekly launch readiness review workflow

title: Knowledge Files
body:
Knowledge Files

Knowledge files let you upload documents that your assistant can reference when answering questions. Upload farm manuals, crop plans, insurance documents, or any other material you want your assistant to know about.

Supported formats

| Format | Extension | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| PDF | .pdf | Text extracted automatically (with OCR fallback for scanned documents) |
| Word Document | .docx | Full text extraction |
| Plain Text | .txt | Direct text |
| Markdown | .md | Supports frontmatter |

Limits

| Limit | Value |
|---|---|
| Max file size (upload) | 10 MB |
| Max parsed content per file | 500 KB |
| Max total storage | 2 MB across all files |
| Max number of files | 20 |

Uploading files

1. Go to Settings > Knowledge
2. Drag and drop a file or click to browse
3. Choose the scope: Shared (all agents) or a specific agent
4. Upload

FarmClaw processes the document, extracts the text, sanitizes the content, and indexes it for retrieval. Your assistant can reference the content immediately.

Shared vs. per-agent knowledge

| Scope | Who can access | Best for |
|---|---|---|
| Shared | All agents in your workspace | General farm documents, SOPs, reference material |
| Per-agent | Only the assigned agent | Specialized documents relevant to one agent's role |

You can reassign files between shared and per-agent at any time using the dropdown on each file's card.

If you delete an agent that has per-agent knowledge files, those files are automatically promoted to shared so they remain accessible.

How retrieval works

When your assistant receives a question, it searches your knowledge files using a hybrid approach:

- Semantic search -- Finds content that is conceptually similar to the question using vector embeddings, even if different words are used
- Keyword search -- Matches specific terms and phrases from the question

The most relevant passages are included in the assistant's context when generating a response. Your assistant will cite the source document when using knowledge file content.

Large documents are automatically split into chunks for efficient retrieval. No configuration needed.

Managing knowledge files

From Settings > Knowledge, you can:

- View all uploaded files with their type, size, status, and agent assignment
- Monitor storage -- A progress bar shows your total usage
- Reassign scope -- Move files between shared and per-agent
- Delete files you no longer need
- Review failed uploads -- See error messages and retry or remove

A few well-chosen, relevant documents will give better results than uploading everything you have. Focus on documents your assistant is likely to need when answering questions.

What's next

- Memory Vault -- Persistent facts and context across conversations
- Custom Skills -- Create new capabilities for your assistant
- AI Agents -- Assign knowledge to specific agents

title: Memory Vault
body:
Memory Vault

The Memory Vault gives your assistant persistent memory across conversations. Important facts, preferences, and context are stored and automatically recalled when relevant -- so your assistant doesn't forget what you told it yesterday.

How it works

During conversations, your assistant stores observations in the Memory Vault. Each memory is classified by type and indexed with semantic embeddings for retrieval by meaning.

When your assistant receives a new message, it searches the vault for relevant memories and includes them in its context. This means your assistant can:

- Remember your preferences ("I prefer CHS over ADM for soybeans")
- Recall past decisions ("We decided to hold corn until March")
- Track ongoing situations ("The south quarter flooded last Tuesday")
- Build on previous conversations without re-explaining context

Memory types

| Type | Example |
|---|---|
| Fact | "Farm is 2,500 acres of corn and soybeans" |
| Preference | "Always check CHS bids first" |
| Commitment | "Contracted 50% of corn at $4.80 in January" |
| Outcome | "Corn yielded 180 bu/acre on the north field" |
| Summary | "October harvest recap: 450,000 bushels total" |

Memory tiers

Memories are automatically classified into tiers based on confidence and type:

| Tier | What goes here |
|---|---|
| Knowledge | High-confidence facts and strong preferences |
| Episodic | Conversations, events, and moderate-confidence observations |
| Working | Short-term context, low-confidence items |

Higher-tier memories are prioritized during retrieval.

Retrieval

Your assistant automatically searches the Memory Vault when processing messages. You can also ask directly:

The vault uses hybrid retrieval:
1. Semantic search finds conceptually similar memories using vector embeddings
2. Keyword search matches specific terms
3. Results are merged, scored, and the top matches are included in context

Settings

Configure the Memory Vault from Settings > Memory:

| Setting | Options | Default |
|---|---|---|
| Mode | Native only / Hybrid local vault | Native only |
| Capture level | Conservative / Standard / Aggressive | Standard |
| Retention days | 7--3,650 days | 365 |
| Exclude categories | Comma-separated list (e.g., "secrets, otp") | Empty |
| Auto-checkpoint | On / Off | On |
| Auto-compress | On / Off | On |

Compression and checkpoints

Over time, the vault accumulates many memories. The compression system:

- Groups related memories together
- Deduplicates semantically similar records
- Promotes episodic memories to knowledge tier when confidence increases
- Tombstones low-confidence working-tier records that are no longer useful
- Creates checkpoints so you can review the vault state at any point

Compression runs automatically when enabled, or you can trigger it manually from the settings page.

Diagnostics

The Memory settings page includes a diagnostics panel showing:

- Total records (active and tombstoned)
- Records added in the last 24 hours
- Average confidence score
- Tier distribution breakdown
- Latest record timestamp

Privacy

- Memories are stored per-workspace and never shared between customers
- All memory data is stored with Row-Level Security in your database
- Exact-duplicate detection prevents redundant storage
- You can delete any or all memories at any time

What's next

- Knowledge Files -- Upload reference documents
- Custom Skills -- Create new assistant capabilities
- AI Agents -- Configure your assistant team

title: Schedules & Briefings
body:
Schedules & Briefings

Schedules let you automate recurring messages from your assistant. The main feature is the daily briefing -- a configurable morning summary posted to your Discord channel covering weather, grain bids, and scale ticket activity.

Daily briefing

The briefing is a single scheduled message that combines multiple sections. Configure it from Settings > Briefings.

Briefing sections

Toggle each section on or off:

| Section | What it includes |
|---|---|
| Weather Forecast | Temperature, wind, precipitation, spray windows |
| Grain Bids | Cash bids from configured elevators, basis changes |
| Scale Ticket Summary | Yesterday's deliveries, bushels by crop, load counts |

Configuration

| Setting | Options |
|---|---|
| Enable/disable | Toggle the entire briefing on or off |
| Send time | Time of day (e.g., 06:30) |
| Timezone | America/Chicago, America/Denver, America/New\York, America/Los\Angeles |
| Discord channel | The channel ID where the briefing is posted |

To get a Discord channel ID: enable Developer Mode in Discord (User Settings > Advanced), then right-click the channel and click "Copy Channel ID".

Per-agent cron jobs

In addition to the briefing system, individual agents can have their own scheduled jobs:

| Schedule | Default Time | Required Skill | What it posts |
|---|---|---|---|
| Morning Weather | 6:00 AM daily | Weather | Full morning briefing with forecast, alerts, and spray window assessment |
| Daily Grain Bids | 9:00 AM Mon--Fri | Grain Bids | Cash grain bids from all configured elevators |

Configure per-agent cron jobs from Settings > Agents on each agent's card. Times are in your farm's local timezone.

Cost considerations

Each scheduled run consumes API tokens:

- Morning weather briefing: 1,500--3,000 tokens
- Daily grain bids: 2,000--4,000 tokens
- Full briefing (all sections): 3,000--6,000 tokens

Factor this into your spending cap and usage estimates. Disabling unused schedules is the easiest way to reduce costs.

What's next

- Skills & Scheduling -- Per-agent schedule configuration
- Metered Usage -- Understand how schedules affect costs
- Spending Cap -- Set budget limits

title: Secrets Vault

headings.title: What agents can and cannot see

body:
Secrets Vault

The Secrets Vault lets you store API keys, tokens, and passwords that your agents can use when calling external APIs. Your agents never see the raw credential — they work with opaque handles, and the server injects the real value into HTTP requests behind the scenes.

How it works

Credential injection follows a three-step flow:

1. Store a secret — you add a label, the raw value, and an injection scheme (e.g. Bearer token) in Settings > Secrets Vault.
2. Agent gets a handle — when an agent needs to call an external API, it calls the usecredential tool with the secret's label. It receives an opaque handle ID — not the raw value.
3. Server injects the credential — the agent passes the handle to the httprequest tool. The server resolves the handle, decrypts the secret, injects it into the correct HTTP header, and sends the request. The raw value never enters the LLM context.

Adding a secret

Navigate to Settings > Secrets Vault and click Add Secret.

| Field | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|
| Label | Yes | A short identifier (e.g. climate-fieldview, my-weather-api). Letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores only. |
| Description | No | A human-readable note for your reference. |
| Secret Value | Yes | The raw API key, token, or password. Encrypted immediately — never stored in plaintext. |
| Injection Scheme | Yes | How the credential is attached to outgoing HTTP requests (see table below). |
| Allowed Domains | No | Restrict which hosts this credential can be sent to. |
| Allowed Agents | No | Restrict which agents can request a handle for this secret. |

Injection schemes

| Scheme | HTTP result | Example |
|---|---|---|
| Bearer Token | Authorization: Bearer sk-abc123 | Most REST APIs (OpenAI, Stripe, etc.) |
| Basic Auth | Authorization: Basic base64(user:pass) | APIs using HTTP Basic authentication |
| Custom Header | X-API-Key: sk-abc123 | APIs that use a named header for auth |
| Query Parameter | ?apikey=sk-abc123 | APIs that accept keys in the URL (less common) |

Domain scoping

You can restrict each secret to specific domains. When allowed domains are set, the httprequest tool will refuse to send the credential to any other host.

Always scope credentials to the domains that need them. For example, if your API key is for api.fieldview.com, set that as the allowed domain. This prevents the credential from being sent anywhere else, even if the agent is tricked by a prompt injection.

Agent scoping

If you run multiple agents (e.g. Grain Specialist, Weather Bot), you can restrict which agents can request a handle for each secret. When allowed agents are set, only those agents can use the credential — others will receive an error.

Leave the agent list empty to allow all agents.

Security architecture

Secret values are encrypted at rest using AES-256-GCM with a server-side key. The encrypted ciphertext is never exposed through any API endpoint. Only the masked hint (e.g. sk-a23) is returned to the dashboard.

Key security properties:

- Ephemeral handles — credential handles expire after 60 seconds and can only be consumed once.
- SSRF protection — the httprequest tool blocks requests to private IP ranges and requires HTTPS.
- Response redaction — if a secret value appears in an API response, it is automatically stripped before the response reaches the LLM.
- Audit trail — every handle creation, credential injection, and reveal attempt is logged in tenantsecretauditlog with tenant, agent, and run context.

What agents can and cannot see

| Visible to agent | Not visible to agent |
|---|---|
| Secret label | Raw secret value |
| Injection scheme (e.g. "bearer") | Encrypted ciphertext |
| Handle ID (opaque reference) | Credentials echoed in HTTP responses |
| Allowed domains list | |

Break-glass access

Break-glass access puts the raw secret value directly into the LLM context. This should only be used when credential injection via httprequest is not possible — for example, when the agent needs to pass an API key inside a request body rather than a header.

The revealsecret tool is an escape hatch for cases where handle-based injection is not sufficient. It is:

- Disabled by default — you must explicitly enable it in Settings > Secrets Vault.
- Approval-gated — every call requires owner approval before execution.
- Fully audited — every reveal is logged with the agent, run, and justification reason.
- Redacted post-turn — after the LLM turn completes, revealed values are stripped from stored messages and conversation traces.
- Rate-limited — maximum 5 reveals per hour per tenant.

Only secrets with usagemode set to "Break glass" can be revealed. Standard injection-only secrets cannot be revealed even if the tool is enabled.

FAQ

Can my agent leak my API key?
No, under normal operation. The usecredential → httprequest flow keeps the raw value entirely server-side. The agent only sees a handle ID. Even if the agent's conversation is compromised, the attacker gets an opaque handle that expires in 60 seconds, not the actual credential. If you enable break-glass access, the raw value does enter the LLM context for that turn, but it is redacted from stored messages afterward.

What if I delete a secret mid-conversation?
Any outstanding handles for that secret will fail when the httprequest tool tries to consume them. The agent will receive an error message and can inform the user.

How many secrets can I store?
There is no hard limit on the number of secrets per workspace. Each secret adds negligible storage overhead.

Are secrets included in my subscription?
Yes. The Secrets Vault is included in all FarmClaw plans at no additional cost. Only the API calls made through httprequest count toward your usage metering.

What's next

- Custom Skills — build skills that use your stored credentials
- Knowledge Files — upload reference documents for your agents
- Email — send and receive email through your assistant

title: Workflow KPI Review Cadence
body:
Workflow KPI Review Cadence

Run a weekly launch review every Monday at 16:00 UTC.

Required KPIs

- Time to first publish (median)
- Draft to publish completion rate
- Run success rate
- Retry rate
- Retry recovery success rate
- Approval cycle time (p50)
- Weekly active builders
- Estimated operator hours saved

Meeting agenda

1. Check KPI trend deltas week-over-week.
2. Review performance gates and sample sufficiency.
3. Decide rollout action: promote, hold, or rollback ring.
4. Assign follow-up owners and due dates.

Escalation thresholds

Escalate to incident response if:

- run success rate drops by 10+ points week-over-week
- retry recovery falls below 70%
- approval cycle p50 doubles week-over-week
- any gate remains failed across two reviews

title: Workflow Builder Rollout Rings
body:
Workflow Builder Rollout Rings

FarmClaw uses three rollout rings to release workflow builder updates safely:

- Canary: first 10% of customers by deterministic customer-ID bucket.
- Standard: next 60% of customers.
- Delayed: remaining 30% of customers after gates pass.

Target ring control

The environment variable WORKFLOWBUILDERROLLOUTTARGETRING controls how far release is opened:

- canary: canary customers only.
- standard: canary + standard.
- delayed: all rings.

If unset or invalid, FarmClaw defaults to delayed.

Promotion checklist

Before promoting to the next ring:

1. Check launch-readiness dashboard for KPI regressions.
2. Confirm performance gates (INP, LCP, CLS) are passing or have an approved exception.
3. Confirm no P1/P2 workflow incidents are open.
4. Confirm retry recovery and approval cycle metrics are stable week-over-week.

Rollback rule

If P1 incidents are detected or gate failures persist across two checks, set
WORKFLOWBUILDERROLLOUTTARGETRING back to the prior ring and hold promotion
until runbook actions are complete.

title: Visual Workflow Builder
body:
Visual Workflow Builder

Use FarmClaw's visual workflow builder to design automations without calling APIs.

Guided walkthrough

1. Open Settings > Workflows (/settings/workflows).
2. Select New workflow and choose a starter template.
3. Add or edit nodes in the canvas, then resolve validation errors in the right panel.
4. Click Publish to promote the current draft and trigger runtime deploy.
5. Click Run or open Runs to verify execution and artifacts.
6. Open Launch readiness to review rollout ring and KPI baseline.

Keyboard map

- Cmd/Ctrl + S save draft
- Cmd/Ctrl + Z undo
- Shift + Cmd/Ctrl + Z redo
- Cmd/Ctrl + D duplicate selected node
- Cmd/Ctrl + K open command palette
- F fit canvas
- + / - zoom
- Esc clear selection / close palette

Recovery controls

- Retry from here: retry a failed/canceled run from a specific step.
- Rerun workflow: queue a fresh run for the latest published version.
- Rollback: move published version to the prior release and redeploy.

Related docs

- Workflow rollout rings
- Workflow builder support runbook

title: Common Issues

headings.title: I didn't receive the magic link email
The magic link says it's expired
I'm getting redirected to the login page repeatedly
The bot appears offline in Discord
The bot is online but not responding
Photo OCR didn't read my ticket correctly
Can't find a ticket I logged
I was charged but my assistant isn't working
How do I get a refund?

body:
Login & authentication

I didn't receive the magic link email

- Check your spam, junk, or promotions folder
- Verify you entered the correct email address
- Wait 60 seconds before requesting another link — there's a cooldown
- If using a corporate email, your IT department may be blocking the sender
- Try a different email provider (Gmail, Outlook) if issues persist

The magic link says it's expired

Magic links expire after a short time for security. Request a new one from the login page. Don't bookmark or save magic link URLs — they're single-use.

I'm getting redirected to the login page repeatedly

Your session may have expired. Clear your browser cookies for FarmClaw and request a new magic link. If this happens frequently, check that your browser allows cookies.

---

Discord connectivity

The bot appears offline in Discord

1. Check your dashboard — verify your workspace shows "Active" status
2. Check that the FarmClaw bot hasn't been kicked from the server. Re-add it from Settings > Discord
3. Try sending a DM to the bot to rule out channel permission issues

The bot is online but not responding

- Make sure you're messaging in a channel the bot has access to. Check the bot's role permissions in Discord server settings.
- Verify the bot has these permissions: Send Messages, Read Message History, Read Messages/View Channels, Embed Links, Attach Files
- If using channel bindings, make sure the channel you're in is bound to an agent or is handled by the default agent
- Check your dashboard for any error indicators

The bot responds slowly

- First response in a new conversation may take a few extra seconds
- Grain bid fetches involve scraping elevator websites, which can take 5–15 seconds per elevator
- Photo OCR for scale tickets requires image processing, which takes a few extra seconds
- If consistently slow, check your usage dashboard for any unusual patterns

The first response in a conversation may be slightly slower as your assistant loads context. Subsequent responses will be faster.

---

Grain bids

"Elevator website unreachable" error

- The elevator's website may be temporarily down — try again in a few minutes
- The elevator may have changed their website URL. Check their current bid page URL and update it in Settings > Farm > Elevators
- Some elevator websites have anti-scraping protections that may block automated access

Bids seem outdated

- Cash grain bids are typically updated once daily in the morning by elevators
- If you check in the evening, the bids shown are from that morning's posting
- FarmClaw always fetches fresh data — it never uses cached or remembered prices from previous conversations

Missing crops in bid results

- Verify the missing crop is listed in the elevator's configuration. Go to Settings > Farm > Elevators and check the crops list for that elevator
- Not all elevators post bids for all commodities — the elevator may not buy that crop at that location
- Seasonal crops may not have posted bids during off-seasons

---

Weather

"Unable to fetch forecast" error

- The National Weather Service API occasionally has outages. Try again in a few minutes.
- Verify your weather coordinates are correct in Settings > Farm. Latitude should be 40–49°N and longitude should be -104 to -89°W for the supported Midwest region.

Morning briefing not appearing

- Check that the morning weather cron job is enabled for one of your agents in Settings > Agents
- The briefing is scheduled for 6:00 AM in your configured timezone. Verify your timezone is correct.
- Check the channel that the agent with the cron job is bound to — the briefing posts there

Spray window data seems wrong

Spray window assessment uses these thresholds:
- Wind: 3–10 mph (too calm = inversion risk, too high = drift risk)
- Temperature: 50–85°F
- No precipitation in current hour or next 2 hours
- Humidity: below 80%

If the assessment doesn't match your conditions, note that it's based on the NWS hourly forecast, not real-time sensor data. Local conditions may vary from the forecast.

---

Scale tickets

Photo OCR didn't read my ticket correctly

- Take the photo in good lighting with the ticket flat and fully visible
- Avoid glare, shadows, and wrinkled tickets
- Handwritten tickets are harder to read than printed ones — consider using voice or structured entry instead
- After OCR extraction, you're always shown the results for confirmation. Reply with corrections before saving.

Can't find a ticket I logged

- Use "show today's tickets" or "show tickets from [date]" to search by date
- Check that you're asking the right agent if you have multiple agents — scale tickets are stored per workspace, but only agents with the scale-tickets skill can query them

Export not working

The export function generates files from your scale ticket data. If exports fail, try again or contact support if the issue persists.

---

Setup

Workspace setup seems stuck

Workspace creation typically completes in a few seconds. If it takes longer:

- Refresh your dashboard page
- Check that your subscription is active
- Try logging out and back in

If the issue persists for more than a few minutes, contact support.

Workspace shows an error

Common causes:

- The FarmClaw bot may have been removed from your Discord server -- re-add it from Settings > Discord
- Your subscription may have lapsed -- check Settings > Billing

---

Billing

I was charged but my assistant isn't working

- Check your workspace status on the dashboard first
- If the workspace shows an error, try the setup troubleshooting steps above
- Your subscription is valid regardless of workspace status -- you won't lose your billing period

How do I get a refund?

Contact support through your dashboard. Refund requests are handled on a case-by-case basis.

Unexpected high usage charges

Check your usage dashboard at Settings > Usage:

- Look for spikes in daily token usage
- Automated cron jobs (morning weather, daily grain bids) consume tokens every day even if you don't read them
- Multiple agents each consume tokens independently
- Photo OCR uses significantly more tokens than text-based interactions

To reduce usage, consider disabling unused cron jobs and using text entry instead of photo OCR when possible. See the Metered Usage guide for more tips.

title: Troubleshooting
body:
Getting help

If you run into issues with FarmClaw, this section covers the most common problems and their solutions. For issues not covered here, contact support through your dashboard.

Quick diagnostics

Before diving into specific issues, check these basics:

1. Workspace status — Check your dashboard. Your workspace should show "Active" status.
2. Discord bot — Verify the bot appears online (green dot) in your Discord server's member list.
3. Subscription — Confirm your subscription is active in Settings > Billing.

If all three check out and you're still having issues, read on for specific scenarios.

Common categories

- Common Issues — Login problems, Discord connectivity, skill errors, billing questions, and more
- Workflow Builder Support Runbook — Incident triage and mitigation for workflow publish/run failures

title: Workflow Builder Support Runbook
body:
Workflow Builder Support Runbook

Use this runbook when workflow builder behavior impacts operators or production runs.

Severity model

- P1: publish failures or run failures across multiple customers with active impact.
- P2: single-customer publish/run failures that block operations.
- P3: degraded UX with available workaround.

First-response checklist (first 15 minutes)

1. Confirm incident scope: customer IDs, instance IDs, workflow IDs, run IDs.
2. Check /settings/workflows/launch for KPI spikes and gate failures.
3. Validate rollout target ring and customer assigned ring.
4. Inspect latest run timeline, step errors, and artifact events.
5. Classify incident severity and assign incident commander.

API checks

- GET /api/instances/:id/workflow-runs
- GET /api/instances/:id/workflow-runs/:runId
- POST /api/instances/:id/workflow-runs/:runId/retry-step
- POST /api/instances/:id/workflows/:workflowId/rollback

Immediate mitigations

- Pause ring promotion by setting WORKFLOWBUILDERROLLOUTTARGETRING to prior ring.
- For run-time regressions, rollback affected workflows to prior published version.
- For validation regressions, block publish and route users to manual run procedures.

Escalation path

1. Support on-call
2. Workflow platform engineer
3. Runtime/infrastructure on-call
4. Product owner for customer comms

Incident close-out

1. Document root cause and recovery timeline.
2. Link impacted run IDs and workflows.
3. Update weekly KPI review agenda with follow-up actions.
4. Add regression coverage for the triggering condition.
